scenarios.title: Bulk RNA-Seq Differential Expression Analysis
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks involving PyDESeq2, DESeq2-like Python differential expression analysis, raw count and metadata validation, bulk RNA-seq contrasts, Wald summaries, LFC shrinkage, or VST.
scenarios.role: Use PyDESeq2 as a Python package for bulk RNA-seq differential expression workflows from data validation through fitted models and statistical results.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions PyDESeq2, pydeseq2, DeseqDataSet, DeseqStats, DESeq2 in Python, bulk RNA-seq counts, count matrix and metadata, formula designs, contrasts, Wald tests, padj, log2FoldChange, lfc_shrink, VST, or Cooks outliers.
scenarios.best_for: Preparing PyDESeq2 inputs, running DeseqDataSet.deseq2 workflows, computing DeseqStats results, interpreting contrasts and result tables, troubleshooting count/design errors, and inspecting normalization/VST internals.
scenarios.avoid_when: Use single-cell omics skills for Scanpy/scvi single-cell workflows, general pandas/AnnData skills for non-DEA data wrangling, R/Bioconductor guidance for unsupported DESeq2-only features, and Python repository maintenance for editing PyDESeq2 source code.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `pydeseq2` when the user is using PyDESeq2 or asks for DESeq2-like bulk RNA-seq analysis in Python. Start at the root router, then pick data I/O before model construction, DEA workflows for fitting, statistics/results after fitting, or model-fitting internals for staged normalization/VST/debugging.
